content.text:
<timestamp>Wednesday, Jun 10, 2026, 10:30 AM (UTC+10)</timestamp>
<user_query>
is this prompt still in use? ARCHITECT_PROMPT_NAME = "domain-driven/architect"

</user_query>
I’ll check where `ARCHITECT_PROMPT_NAME` and the literal `domain-driven/architect` are referenced across the repo to confirm whether it’s still actively used.

Yes — it is still in use.

`ARCHITECT_PROMPT_NAME = "domain-driven/architect"` is actively referenced in runtime code:

- `agents/domain_driven/langfuse_prompts.py` uses it when calling `prompt_text(...)` inside `_fetch_domain_driven_prompts(...)`.
- That mapping is consumed by `agents/domain_driven/runner.py` via `fetch_domain_driven_prompts_for_credentials(...)` to substitute `PROMPT_DOMAIN_DRIVEN_ARCHITECT` in both domain workflows.
- The placeholder appears in both workflow packs: `workflows/domain-bootstrap/workflow.yaml` and `workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml`.
- It’s also part of prompt management/tests (`scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`, `tests/agents/domain_driven/test_langfuse_prompts.py`).

So unless the whole domain-driven runner path is dead (it isn’t, based on current wiring), this prompt name is live.
I’ll check for both `domain-designer` and `domain-driven/designer` references to verify whether that prompt is wired into runtime or just docs/upload files.

`domain-designer` is **not** in active runtime use in this codebase.

What exists now:

- Active runtime prompt is `domain-driven/architect` (used by `agents/domain_driven/langfuse_prompts.py` and the domain-driven runner path).
- `domain-driven/domain-designer` appears only in `scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py` as a **legacy migration source** that gets copied to `domain-driven/domain-designer-legacy`.
- There are also `.cursor` skill references named `domain-designer`, but those are agent-skill docs, not runtime Langfuse prompt resolution.

So: **`domain-designer` is legacy/compat plumbing, not a live prompt key used by the domain-driven runner.**

<timestamp>Wednesday, Jun 10, 2026, 10:32 AM (UTC+10)</timestamp>
<user_query>
ok remove any prompt upload scripts we have in the repository.
</user_query>
I’ll locate every script in `scripts/` that uploads prompts (not runtime prompt code), then delete those files and quickly check for obvious broken imports/usages.
I found two upload scripts only; I’m deleting both now, then I’ll verify there are no prompt-upload scripts left under `scripts/`.

Removed all prompt-upload scripts from the repo.

Deleted files:
- `scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`
- `scripts/langfuse_consolidate_concierge_prompts.py`

Verified `scripts/` now has no prompt upload logic (`create_prompt`, upload/consolidate prompt patterns are gone).

One thing to note: docs still reference those script names (for example `domain-driven/spec.md`). If you want, I can clean those references next so docs stop pointing to removed files.
I’ll remove stale references to those deleted scripts and update the wording so the docs no longer instruct running non-existent upload tooling.
I’m updating `domain-driven/spec.md` now to remove the deleted-script references and replace them with script-agnostic upload guidance so the spec stays valid.

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /workspaces/agno/domain-driven/spec.md
@@
-| **Langfuse prompt upload from local files** | `scripts/langfuse_consolidate_concierge_prompts.py` (`_load_langfuse_env`, `client.create_prompt(name, prompt, labels=[_label()], type="text", commit_message=...)`). | that script | Copy into a new upload script; see §6. |
+| **Langfuse prompt upload from local files** | Use the Langfuse API/SDK to publish prompt files as versioned text prompts with label `${LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL:-production}`. | Langfuse API contract | No repository upload script required. |
@@
-## 6. Upload script (new, but copied from an existing one)
-
-Add `scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`, modeled directly on `scripts/langfuse_consolidate_concierge_prompts.py`:
-
-- Reuse its `_load_langfuse_env()` (reads `.env`), `_client()`, `_label()`, and `_exists()` helpers verbatim.
-- For each local prompt: read the file, strip the leading HTML-comment header (the `<!-- Langfuse name: ... -->` block), and `client.create_prompt(name=<langfuse name>, prompt=<body>, labels=[_label()], type="text", commit_message=...)`.
-- Upload order: `_architecture` and `_engineering-stance` **first**, then the three role prompts (architect, design-reviewer, coder) which reference them. (`design-reviewer` also references the existing `architecture-design/reviewer`, which is already uploaded.)
-- Idempotency: `create_prompt` creates a new version each call; that is acceptable and matches the consolidate script. Skipping unchanged bodies is a nice-to-have, not required.
-
-Run: `uv run python scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`.
+## 6. Prompt publication process
+
+Publish prompt files to Langfuse outside this repository (CI job, external automation, or manual API/SDK invocation):
+
+- Read each local prompt file, strip the leading HTML-comment header (`<!-- Langfuse name: ... -->`), and upload with `create_prompt(name=<langfuse name>, prompt=<body>, labels=[<label>], type="text", commit_message=...)`.
+- Publish in dependency order: `_architecture` and `_engineering-stance` **first**, then the role prompts (architect, design-reviewer, coder) that reference shared blocks.
+- Versioning behavior: `create_prompt` creates a new version per publish; that is acceptable. Optional optimization: skip unchanged bodies.
+- Required environment remains `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_BASE_URL`, and optional `LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL`.
@@
-2. **Before a run**, the prompts are uploaded to Langfuse (§6). The Langfuse copy, at label `${LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL:-production}`, is the runtime source.
+2. **Before a run**, the prompts are published to Langfuse (§6). The Langfuse copy, at label `${LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL:-production}`, is the runtime source.
@@
-- `scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py` — copy of the consolidate script (§6).
*** End Patch

content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /workspaces/agno/domain-driven/spec.md
@@
-| **Langfuse prompt upload from local files** | `scripts/langfuse_consolidate_concierge_prompts.py` (`_load_langfuse_env`, `client.create_prompt(name, prompt, labels=[_label()], type="text", commit_message=...)`). | that script | Copy into a new upload script; see §6. |
+| **Langfuse prompt upload from local files** | Use Langfuse API/SDK prompt versioning (`create_prompt(name, prompt, labels=[label], type="text", commit_message=...)`). | Langfuse API contract | No repository upload script required; see §6. |
@@
-2. **Before a run**, the prompts are uploaded to Langfuse (§6). The Langfuse copy, at label `${LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL:-production}`, is the runtime source.
+2. **Before a run**, the prompts are published to Langfuse (§6). The Langfuse copy, at label `${LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL:-production}`, is the runtime source.
@@
-## 6. Upload script (new, but copied from an existing one)
-
-Add `scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`, modeled directly on `scripts/langfuse_consolidate_concierge_prompts.py`:
-
-- Reuse its `_load_langfuse_env()` (reads `.env`), `_client()`, `_label()`, and `_exists()` helpers verbatim.
-- For each local prompt: read the file, strip the leading HTML-comment header (the `<!-- Langfuse name: ... -->` block), and `client.create_prompt(name=<langfuse name>, prompt=<body>, labels=[_label()], type="text", commit_message=...)`.
-- Upload order: `_architecture` and `_engineering-stance` **first**, then the three role prompts (architect, design-reviewer, coder) which reference them. (`design-reviewer` also references the existing `architecture-design/reviewer`, which is already uploaded.)
-- Idempotency: `create_prompt` creates a new version each call; that is acceptable and matches the consolidate script. Skipping unchanged bodies is a nice-to-have, not required.
-
-Run: `uv run python scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`.
+## 6. Prompt publication process
+
+Publish prompt files to Langfuse outside this repository (CI job, external automation, or manual API/SDK invocation):
+
+- For each local prompt, read the file, strip the leading HTML-comment header (`<!-- Langfuse name: ... -->`), then call `create_prompt(name=<langfuse name>, prompt=<body>, labels=[<label>], type="text", commit_message=...)`.
+- Publish in dependency order: `_architecture` and `_engineering-stance` first, then the role prompts (architect, design-reviewer, coder) that reference those shared blocks.
+- `create_prompt` creates a new version per publish; this is acceptable. Skipping unchanged bodies is optional.
+- Required environment remains `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_BASE_URL`, with `LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL` as the label selector.
@@
-- `scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py` — copy of the consolidate script (§6).
*** End Patch

content.text:
Cleaned up the docs to match the script removals.

Updated `domain-driven/spec.md` to:
- Remove direct references to deleted files:
  - `scripts/langfuse_upload_domain_driven_prompts.py`
  - `scripts/langfuse_consolidate_concierge_prompts.py`
- Replace “upload script” language with a script-agnostic **prompt publication process** via Langfuse API/SDK.
- Keep the operational requirements intact (header stripping, publish order, env vars, versioning behavior).
- Remove the deleted upload script from the “new code inventory.”

Also verified there are no remaining references to those script names anywhere in the repo.
